steps.title: Link the clause to an explanation
steps.content: The words before the blank form a complete clause: astronomers detected a phenomenon 160 million light-years from Earth. The words after the blank form a noun phrase that identifies that phenomenon as the explosion of a massive star.

steps.title: Choose the needed boundary
steps.content: Choice A leaves the noun phrase after the period as a fragment. Choice B creates a comma splice by adding 'it was' after a comma. Choice D uses a semicolon before a noun phrase, but semicolons join independent clauses. A colon correctly introduces the explanatory noun phrase. The answer is C.
